categories.label: Foundation
categories.description: Cross-cutting guard every other skill loads first, plus the project→client pin it enforces.

categories.label: Utilities
categories.description: Cross-cutting infrastructure the workflows hand off to.

categories.label: Setup & connection
categories.description: Get the plugin installed, the data connection authenticated, and updates flowing — run before the onboarding guides.

categories.label: Onboarding (operating model)
categories.description: Operational getting-started guides — teach the project-per-client model and wire up each client's discovery, schedule, and delivery.

categories.label: Tier 1 — daily / monthly workhorses
categories.description: The core close and cleanup workflows run on a routine cadence.

categories.label: Tier 2 — high-value, well-scoped
categories.description: Focused AR / AP / vendor / reporting / briefing workflows.

categories.label: Monitors — read-only recurring watches
categories.description: Strictly read-only watches built to be scheduled; they surface and route, never write.

releaseNotes: 0.12.0 — Client pinning: enforce project↔client matching. The plugin now ships session-guard HOOKS (SessionStart / PreToolUse / PostToolUse) that hard-enforce the project-per-client model: when a project carries a pin file (.claude/quanto-client.json), QuantoBooks data tools are blocked until switch_client to the pinned client has been verified that session, switch_client to any other client is blocked outright, and the pin is announced at session start so Claude switches correctly on its own. New quanto-project-pin skill (Foundation) walks through creating, changing, and removing a pin, and quanto-ops-client-onboarding now pins the project as part of its anchor step. The hooks also AUTO-ALLOW read-only QuantoBooks tools (everything except *_create/*_update/*_delete, authenticate, switch_client) so reads stop prompting for permission; writes keep the normal approval flow. Server-side counterparts (MCP server): multi-client sessions now start with NO active client instead of an auto-picked default (single-client firms still auto-select; data tools return a clear switch_client instruction until one is chosen); every tool response now ends with an [active_client: …] echo line; all tools carry readOnlyHint/destructiveHint annotations; and connections can be PINNED to one client via ?client_id=<id> on the connector URL (or x-quanto-client-id header) — a pinned connection starts on that client and rejects switching away, enforcing the pin on every surface including plain claude.ai chat. quanto-client-context documents the pin short-circuit and the active-client echo. 0.11.0 — Setup & connection skills. Three new ops skills cover the getting-started lifecycle that previously lived only as docs: `quanto-ops-setup` (the standalone bootstrap orchestrator — install the plugin, connect, verify, turn on auto-update, then hand off to firm/client onboarding; designed to be downloaded on its own from the dashboard Learn Center), `quanto-ops-connect` (the QuantoBooks account + data connection: one-click OAuth or API key, confirm the active client, and verify headless/scheduled-run auth — the prerequisite `quanto-schedule-workflow` depends on), and `quanto-ops-update` (update the plugin including the Cowork catalog-cache refresh / remove-re-add / restart cascade). `quanto-ops-firm-onboarding` now points at `quanto-ops-setup` as its prerequisite. 0.10.0 — Local-first scheduling. `quanto-schedule-workflow` now prefers the always-on sandbox's local in-session cron (`CronCreate`, durable) over Claude-managed/cloud routines, and every schedule is a self-rearming chain of one-shot jobs: each fire re-arms the next within a sub-7-day window, so schedules survive CronCreate's 7-day auto-expiry — which otherwise kills a weekly cadence after one run and never fires a monthly one at all. The scheduled-prompt template now carries the re-arm step, a run-or-keep-alive-hop gate, and a `# quanto-schedule:` marker line for CronList/CronDelete management; managed/host routines (`/schedule`, Cowork tasks) remain the documented fallback for ephemeral sessions. `quanto-ops-firm-onboarding` notes the local self-rearming scheduler model. Review-only and headless-auth safety are unchanged. 0.9.0 — Onboarding & delivery. Three new skills that make the project-per-client operating model explicit and close the orchestration loop so automated work actually reaches the firm. (1) quanto-ops-firm-onboarding — the firm-level getting-started guide: teaches the one-project-per-client / one-firm-home-context model, inventories clients via list_clients, sets firm-wide defaults once (delivery destinations, default cadences, headless-auth check), schedules the cross-client quanto-firm-digest, and tracks setup progress in a visible quanto-firm-setup.md checklist it re-reads across conversations so a firm can onboard its clients over multiple sittings. (2) quanto-ops-client-onboarding — the per-client operational counterpart, run inside each client's project: anchors the project to the client, runs quanto-client-onboarding-review as its discovery step, tours the day-to-day toolkit, pins the client's standing schedule (briefing before the call, weekly triage, monthly close) via quanto-schedule-workflow, and wires delivery. Distinct from the diagnostic quanto-client-onboarding-review, which it invokes — operational setup vs books audit. (3) quanto-deliver-results — the delivery half of the loop: sends a workflow's output to Slack, Notion, or email instead of leaving it in the chat, with a hard internal-vs-client-facing line (internal delivery is safe even unattended; client-facing sends and any write stay human-in-the-loop). The foundation guard (quanto-client-context) gains a 'close the loop' rule so every deliverable-producing workflow offers to schedule AND deliver, and quanto-schedule-workflow now captures a delivery destination and bakes it into the scheduled prompt — a scheduled run delivers its read-only summary to an internal firm destination so the report is waiting where the firm works. New 'Onboarding & setup' category; quanto-deliver-results joins Utilities. Skills only — no new MCP tools, minMcpServerVersion unchanged. 0.8.0 — Bundle the QuantoBooks MCP server connection into the plugin. Installing the plugin in Claude Code now wires up the live tool connection (qbo_*, quanto_*, karbon_*), not just the skills: a bundled `.mcp.json` registers the hosted QuantoBooks MCP server (https://mcp.quantobooks.com/) as an HTTP connector, and Claude Code runs the one-click OAuth sign-in on first use — no API key to generate or paste, and write tools are gated by the qbo.write consent scope. This applies to the Claude Code CLI plugin path; the curl/npx loose-file installers still copy skills only (they can't carry a connector), so those users connect the server manually. An API-key header config (Authorization: Bearer ${QUANTOBOOKS_API_KEY}) remains a documented fallback. 0.7.0 — Automation pack: five new read-only, schedule-friendly skills built for clients automating their recurring workflows. (1) quanto-client-briefing — the internal-facing mirror of quanto-management-report: a pre-call brief fanning across QBO financial movement (incl. cash trajectory), Quanto review flags, Karbon work items / notes / profile, and recent knowledge-base documents, scoped to a 'since last call' window — bottom line, what changed, what to raise, what the client might ask, open follow-ups. Leans into the loop harder than any other skill, re-offering to schedule itself before every standing call until a cadence is pinned. (2) quanto-cash-flow-watch — weekly cash position, net burn/build, expected AR-in vs AP-out, and a directional runway estimate. (3) quanto-spend-watch — proactive between-close monitor for new vendors, unusually large or duplicate payments, and subscription creep. (4) quanto-missing-docs-chase — documentation-completeness audit that finds unsupported transactions and drafts a client-ready open-items / PBC request list. (5) quanto-firm-digest — the one intentionally firm-scoped skill: a cross-client morning triage that sweeps every client (read-only) and ranks where attention is needed today, deliberately overriding the single-active-client rule to route the firm_user to the right client + skill. All five are strictly read-only and offer to schedule themselves via quanto-schedule-workflow, whose relationship list now includes them. 0.6.0 — Knowledge-base search. New quanto_knowledge_base_search MCP tool: one semantic search across the entire knowledge base — uploaded documents, Google Drive files, firm-wide SOPs, and Karbon notes/work items/profiles — spanning both client and firm scope, returning the matching chunks. quanto-document-lookup and the quanto-client-context guard are restructured to a search-first → drill-in flow: always search first, then fetch the full source document with quanto_document_get / quanto_firm_document_get / karbon_*_get using the document_id carried on each hit. quanto_document_query / quanto_firm_document_query gain source and has_text filters so a completed Google Drive copy is distinguishable from an upload that is still processing or stuck. 0.5.0 — Cowork + visuals + recurring runs + project-per-client. (1) Cowork install: add the marketplace from Customize → Personal plugins → + → Add marketplace (Cowork doesn't take /plugin slash commands; Claude Code CLI still does). (2) New quanto-report-templates skill: QuantoBooks-themed, self-contained HTML dashboards (management report, financial period) that report skills fill with MCP data and render — inline in Cowork, as a saved .html in Claude Code. (3) New quanto-schedule-workflow skill: per-client recurring runs (e.g. AR every Monday for one client) via the host's scheduler, pinned to a specific client_id and REVIEW-ONLY — an unattended run prepares + summarizes, never writes/pays/sends; flags that scheduled runs need the connector authenticated non-interactively. The cadence-appropriate workflows (ar-followup, flag-triage, ap-pay-run, transaction-cleanup, month-end-close, balance-sheet-review, management-report) offer to schedule themselves. (4) quanto-client-context now reflects the project-per-client setup: with no client named it infers from the project/workspace name (match against list_clients) and proposes-and-confirms, falling back to asking explicitly when there's no confident match. 0.4.0 — Ship the bundle as a Claude Code plugin marketplace. Claude Code / Cowork users can now install ALL skills with two commands (`/plugin marketplace add quantotechnologylabs/quantobooks-skills` then `/plugin install quantobooks@quantobooks`) instead of copying skills one by one; skills appear namespaced as `quantobooks:quanto-*` and update via `/plugin update`. The curl/npx loose-file installer still works for `~/.claude/skills/`. Claude Desktop does NOT support custom plugin marketplaces or filesystem skill folders today — the installer no longer pretends to install there; use the in-app Skills uploader or the downloadable zip instead. 0.3.0 — Prefix every skill with `quanto-` so QuantoBooks skills stay identifiable when users have many skills installed from different sources. Directory names + YAML `name:` frontmatter + all cross-skill references updated. NOTE: if you were installed at v0.2.0, the old unprefixed directories will be left behind in your skills folder — re-run the installer to clean them up (or delete the unprefixed copies by hand). 0.2.0 — Karbon practice-management context. The foundation guard (quanto-client-context) now documents the karbon_* tool tier (client profile, work items, notes, note comments) as an optional, read-only context source for mapped clients. quanto-client-onboarding-review, quanto-document-lookup, quanto-flag-triage, quanto-management-report, and quanto-month-end-close gained conditional Karbon steps so they fold in client profile / engagement / relationship context when present. All additive and best-effort — skills skip Karbon silently when a client isn't mapped. 0.1.0 — Initial skill bundle: foundation client-context guard + 13 workflow skills.
